stories.headline: Magnitude 7.5 earthquake strikes northern Japan; tsunami warning issued

stories.headline: U.S. Fires on Iranian Cargo Ship Touska in Arabian Sea
stories.summary: The military said it had disabled the vessel after it ignored repeated warnings to stop, amid a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. Marines boarded the ship and seized it.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises After US Seizes Iran-Flagged Ship | Bloomberg Brief 4/20/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US Navy attacked and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. Iran says it has no plans to attend potential negotiations with the US in Pakistan with the ceasefire expiring on Tuesday. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses the market volatility amid renewed uncertainties around the war.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Japan issues tsunami warning after powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: Authorities have urged residents of the affected areas to seek higher ground, heed safety instructions and exercise caution.

stories.headline: Moment US navy fires on and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
stories.summary: US military footage shows a destroyer firing warning shots before boarding and seizing an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it tried to evade a naval blockade, amid heightened tensions near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.headline: Oil rebounds as Iran threatens to retaliate for US attack on cargo ship

stories.headline: US captures Iranian ship Touska amid mediation efforts: All we know

stories.headline: As barbed wire blocks kids from class, Palestinians stage ‘Freedom School’
stories.summary: Children from Umm al-Khair in the occupied West Bank protest after settlers erect a fence blocking access to school.

stories.headline: UAE, US Discuss a Potential Financial Lifeline: WSJ
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates has reportedly opened talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the country plunges into further crisis due to the war on Iran, according to the Wall Street Journal. Kasia Klimasinska reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

WWII bomb destroyed in controlled explosion in Paris
stories.summary: Video provided by French police shows the controlled detonation of a large bomb dating from the Second World War in Colombes. After attempts to remove the bomb’s detonator failed at midday, authorities buried it and later destroyed it in a controlled detonation.

stories.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

stories.headline: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
stories.summary: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

stories.headline: Hormuz crisis delivers a Taiwan Strait wake-up call: 5 things to know

stories.summary: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

stories.headline: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.headline: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries have responded to rising oil prices

stories.headline: Wall Street Strategists Bullish on the US Earnings Outlook
stories.summary: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are upbeat on the outlook for US earnings after a positive start to the first-quarter reporting season.

stories.headline: Broker Groww’s Profit Tops Estimates After 100% Post-IPO Surge
stories.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that was above estimates, as investors seek growth to justify a 100% jump in the stock since its initial public offering — a surge that has made it the world’s most expensive brokerage.

stories.headline: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
stories.summary: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

stories.headline: Markets Still Have a Lot of Hope and Optimism, Says NewEdge’s Dawson
stories.summary: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, explains what there is to still like about markets and sees earnings growth as idiosyncratic and not broad based. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UnitedHealth Isn’t Out of the Woods With $6 Billion Medicare Hit
stories.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s results Tuesday will reflect the full effect of Medicare payment changes set in motion three years ago that cost the company billions, cratering profits last year and blindsiding investors.

stories.headline: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazil’s Serra Verde in $2.8 Billion Deal
stories.summary: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a cash-and-stock transaction, adding to a string of recent deals in the industry.

stories.headline: India’s RBI Eases Some Curbs on Banks’ Forex Market Trades
stories.summary: India’s central bank eased some restrictions on banks’ offshore currency market transactions that it had imposed earlier this month to shore up the rupee.

stories.headline: Philippines Outlook Cut to Negative by Fitch on Growth Risks
stories.summary: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying the decline in public investment has created risks to economic growth.

stories.headline: Drop in UK Bond Sales to Limit Political Risk Selloff, Citi Says
stories.summary: A drop in the UK’s planned bond sales may give the debt some support to counter a selloff driven by political risks, according to Citigroup Inc.

stories.headline: Big Names Wait in the Wings as Virginians Decide Their House Maps
stories.summary: With Virginians voting Tuesday to accept or reject redistricting, candidates from both parties await the voters’ judgment to decide whether — or where — to run for Congress.

stories.headline: From Pulpit to Pews, Trump and Pope Are on the Minds of Catholics
stories.summary: At churches with connections to Pope Leo and the Trump administration, pastors and parishioners speak out on the feud between the president and the pontiff.

stories.headline: Planning a hiking trip across Europe? These are the most unspoilt national parks
stories.summary: Bulgaria, Spain, Slovenia and Austria are at the top of the list.

stories.summary: Oil and natural gas prices rose after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.headline: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: Stocks, Bonds Fall as Oil Jumps on Hormuz Setbacks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil jumped, driving US stocks and Treasuries lower after a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on the prospects for peace talks ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
stories.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: Michigan Gas Clerk Helps Save Kidnapped Teen Girl Who Mouthed ‘Help’
stories.summary: A fellow student who had witnessed the kidnapping called the police, and other students helped track the girl to a gas station.

stories.headline: Democrats Eye a Broader Battlefield to Capture Congress in November
stories.summary: Weighed down by President Trump’s approval ratings, some Republican incumbents are struggling to raise money while Democrats look for targets like a Tennessee seat south of Nashville.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.summary: War correspondent, jazz bandleader and impresario, KC Sen shaped Kolkata’s music scene.

stories.headline: Chinese carmaker patents voice-controlled 'in-vehicle toilet'
